---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: abff2ad9a8fc19d2ef7bfa00d335cb7fa88c1276
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-2 stamps `frozen: { at: \u003ctoday\u003e, commit: \u003cHEAD at scaffold time\u003e }` unconditionally on a scaffold that is by definition unauthored, but the workflow parent dc-2 mandates — the human authors every word of the claim after scaffolding — requires editing the file AFTER it carries a frozen stamp. This contradicts the freeze discipline the parent feature itself leans on: parent dc-4's whole architecture (align.FreezeInPlace, PR #99) treats frozen content as preserved verbatim and never re-touched, and story dc-4 cites the same principle ('a frozen artifact must not be touched') to justify its grandfather scope. The precedent story dc-2 invokes (accept and stub-instantiate stamping HEAD at scaffold time) covers verbs that commit their scaffold immediately, so stamp and content coincide; attest explicitly commits nothing, so its frozen.commit records a HEAD whose tree will never contain the bytes the operator eventually authors. The tension is flagged as a judgment call for seam review, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against the parent decision that a machine must not manufacture a human oracle's record — a machine-manufactured freeze stamp over a not-yet-existing human claim is arguably exactly that. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (`verdi attest`) while parent dc-5 settled oq-2 (ADJ-23) on verb parsimony for the closure ritual — 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal... No new verb is added for ac-1.' Attestation authoring is part of the same closure ritual (it produces the evidence close's gate consumes), yet no edge records why ac-2 is exempt from the rationale that consolidated ac-1 into `verdi close`. Parent dc-5's text is explicitly scoped to ac-1 and delegates spelling to the story contract, so this is plausibly deliberate scoping rather than contradiction — but the exemption is inferred, not declared. (confidence 0.20)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/attest-helper#dc-1, spec/attest-helper#dc-2, spec/attest-helper#dc-3, spec/attest-helper#dc-4, spec/attest-helper#dc-5, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-1, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-2, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-3, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-4, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-5] }

This contradicts the freeze discipline the parent feature itself leans on: parent dc-4's whole architecture (align.FreezeInPlace, PR #99) treats frozen content as preserved verbatim and never re-touched, and story dc-4 cites the same principle ('a frozen artifact must not be touched') to justify its grandfather scope. The precedent story dc-2 invokes (accept and stub-instantiate stamping HEAD at scaffold time) covers verbs that commit their scaffold immediately, so stamp and content coincide; attest explicitly commits nothing, so its frozen.commit records a HEAD whose tree will never contain the bytes the operator eventually authors. The tension is flagged as a judgment call for seam review, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against the parent decision that a machine must not manufacture a human oracle's record — a machine-manufactured freeze stamp over a not-yet-existing human claim is arguably exactly that.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (`verdi attest`) while parent dc-5 settled oq-2 (ADJ-23) on verb parsimony for the closure ritual — 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal... ---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-2 stamps `frozen: { at: <today>, commit: <HEAD at scaffold time> }` unconditionally on a scaffold that is by definition unauthored, but the workflow parent dc-2 mandates — the human authors every word of the claim after scaffolding — requires editing the file AFTER it carries a frozen stamp. This contradicts the freeze discipline the parent feature itself leans on: parent dc-4's whole architecture (align.FreezeInPlace, PR #99) treats frozen content as preserved verbatim and never re-touched, and story dc-4 cites the same principle ('a frozen artifact must not be touched') to justify its grandfather scope. The precedent story dc-2 invokes (accept and stub-instantiate stamping HEAD at scaffold time) covers verbs that commit their scaffold immediately, so stamp and content coincide; attest explicitly commits nothing, so its frozen.commit records a HEAD whose tree will never contain the bytes the operator eventually authors. The tension is flagged as a judgment call for seam review, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against the parent decision that a machine must not manufacture a human oracle's record — a machine-manufactured freeze stamp over a not-yet-existing human claim is arguably exactly that. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (`verdi attest`) while parent dc-5 settled oq-2 (ADJ-23) on verb parsimony for the closure ritual — 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal... No new verb is added for ac-1.' Attestation authoring is part of the same closure ritual (it produces the evidence close's gate consumes), yet no edge records why ac-2 is exempt from the rationale that consolidated ac-1 into `verdi close`. Parent dc-5's text is explicitly scoped to ac-1 and delegates spelling to the story contract, so this is plausibly deliberate scoping rather than contradiction — but the exemption is inferred, not declared. (confidence 0.20)
